# Settlement Endpoint Control Tower

Public synthetic reference demo for governing wallet and token endpoints as
settlement instructions before tokenized or fiat value moves.

## Product model

The control tower combines institution identity, legal-entity authority, wallet
and custody context, policy checks, fiat fallback instructions, and ISO
20022/UETR-style evidence into one governed endpoint profile. When a token
endpoint lacks current control evidence, the token route is blocked or held,
the fiat fallback is preserved, and the decision evidence is recorded.

## Page contents

- Landing narrative: the settlement-endpoint problem, the block-and-fallback
  control moment, and the claim boundary.
- Interactive demo console: four operating views, three deterministic synthetic
  scenarios, three role perspectives, client-side synthetic evidence receipt.
- Description of a deeper localhost-only reference application (deterministic
  evaluator, profile lifecycle, repair and evidence refresh, deterministic
  revalidation, append-only decision history through the localhost application
  API with linked decision versions). That application is not hosted here and
  is never called by this page.

## Demo boundary

- Synthetic fixtures only.
- No real payments.
- No live identity or authority verification.
- No live wallet-ownership checks.
- No live sanctions or chain screening.
- No proprietary reference-data rows.
- No production compliance claim.

## Main artifact

- `/` — self-contained browser page with no external network calls.
